amendment.old_rule: effective mass log(G(t)/G(t+1)), plain mean over the declared window t in [3, T/2-1]
amendment.new_rule: cosh (image-corrected) effective mass: solve cosh(m*(T/2-t))/cosh(m*(T/2-t-1)) = G(t)/G(t+1) per t, plain mean over the same declared window t in [3, T/2-1] restricted to the contiguous positive decaying range starting at t=3; unsolvable ratios (<= 1) are dropped from the mean. The same window rule is unchanged.
amendment.reason: The free-field anchor (kappa 0.11 on 16x4^3, cold gauge field) exposed a backward-image bias of the plain log ratio near T/2: the log estimator gives a windowed mass of 0.746 against the exact free value 2*am_q = 0.871, while the cosh estimator plateaus at 0.871 to four digits. The bias inflates the matched free reference and biases S_IR low by construction, independent of any interacting data.
amendment.evidence: test_hybrid_ir_bracket.py::test_s_ir_free_field_round_trip and the free-field plateau check; no interacting ensemble moment, no S_IR value, no hybrid bracket, and no containment comparison had been evaluated at the time of this amendment.
amendment.everything_else_unchanged: envelope factor magnitudes, combination rule, matching rule, Z_V rule, ensembles, bracket construction, and containment rule are unchanged.
